title: scIsoAgent enables autonomous isoform-resolved characterization and sequence-informed interpretation of long-read single-cell transcriptomes
abstract: Alternative isoform usage can alter gene function independently of total gene expression, creating a need to resolve transcript isoforms at single-cell resolution. Long-read single-cell RNA sequencing meets this need by linking cellular identity to transcript isoforms and sequence-level features. Realizing its full biological value requires reproducible workflows that connect specialized long-read analysis with biological interpretation. Existing large language model (LLM)-based biomedical agents support general omics analysis, but are not designed for isoform-resolved long-read single-cell workflows. Here, we present scIsoAgent, an autonomous LLM-powered scientific agent for long-read single-cell RNA-seq analysis. scIsoAgent turns heterogeneous long-read single-cell inputs into traceable isoform-resolved workflows, using stage-aware planning and persistent computational context to support both execution and interpretation. Across complementary evaluations, this design improved the continuity from analysis planning to executable, interactive workflows compared with general-purpose LLM baselines. In real-data reanalysis, scIsoAgent recovered major findings from published long-read single-cell resources and extended a representative differential transcript usage event into a sequence-informed functional hypothesis. By linking full-length isoform sequences with model-inferred transcript properties, scIsoAgent connects observed isoform usage with potential sequence-level functional consequences. These results demonstrate that autonomous scientific agents can transform fragmented long-read single-cell analysis into coherent, reproducible workflows for isoform-resolved discovery and biological interpretation.

paper_title: scIsoAgent enables autonomous isoform-resolved characterization and sequence-informed interpretation of long-read single-cell transcriptomes

objective.answer: The primary objective of the paper is to develop scIsoAgent, an autonomous LLM-powered scientific agent for long-read single-cell RNA-seq analysis, aimed at creating traceable isoform-resolved workflows that connect specialized long-read analysis with biological interpretation.
objective.evidence: Here, we present scIsoAgent, an autonomous LLM-powered scientific agent for long-read single-cell RNA-seq analysis. scIsoAgent turns heterogeneous long-read single-cell inputs into traceable isoform-resolved workflows, using stage-aware planning and persistent computational context to support both execution and interpretation.

knowledge_gap.answer: Existing large language model (LLM)-based biomedical agents support general omics analysis but are not designed for isoform-resolved long-read single-cell workflows, which limits their applicability in this specific domain.
knowledge_gap.evidence: Existing large language model (LLM)-based biomedical agents support general omics analysis, but are not designed for isoform-resolved long-read single-cell workflows.

answer: scIsoAgent provides an adaptive workflow framework for ONT and PacBio-based data, connecting heterogeneous long-read single-cell inputs with isoform-level analysis and sequence-informed biological interpretation.
The design of scIsoAgent includes stage-aware planning, traceable script-based execution, and persistent computational context, which supports reproducible analysis across interactive workflow steps.
scIsoAgent extends isoform-resolved analysis toward sequence-informed interpretation by linking transcript-level analysis results with predictions from RNA sequence models.
evidence: scIsoAgent provides an adaptive workflow framework for ONT and PacBio-based data, connecting heterogeneous long-read single-cell inputs with isoform-level analysis and sequence-informed biological interpretation.
By combining stage-aware planning, traceable script-based execution, and persistent computational context, scIsoAgent supports reproducible analysis across interactive workflow steps.

scIsoAgent further extends isoform-resolved analysis toward sequence-informed interpretation. In this component, scIsoAgent links transcript-level analysis results, including isoform expression, isoform classification, and differential transcript usage, with predictions from RNA sequence models.

inspirational_papers.answer:
- Sahlin and Mäkinen (2021) Accurate spliced alignment of long RNA sequencing reads. (Methodological precursors)
- Kovaka et al. (2019) Transcriptome assembly from long-read RNA-seq alignments with StringTie2. (Experimental baselines)
inspirational_papers.evidence: Representative tools support splice-aware alignment with uLTRA [16], isoform quantification with StringTie2 [17], Mandalorion [18], TALON [19], FLAMES [14], IsoTools [20], and LIQA [21], as well as downstream interpretation and visualization with Swan [22].

steps.step: Construct scIsoAgent as an interactive LLM agent for isoform-resolved long-read single-cell RNA-seq analysis.
steps.input: Heterogeneous project entry points, ranging from raw long-read data to intermediate analysis results.
steps.output: Executable and traceable workflow steps through four operational layers: request interpretation, skill selection, execution control, and interactive session management.
steps.evidence: We first constructed scIsoAgent as an interactive LLM agent for isoform-resolved long-read single-cell RNA-seq analysis. The framework was designed to support heterogeneous project entry points, ranging from raw long-read data to intermediate analysis results.

steps.step: Define the workflow coverage of scIsoAgent across major stages of isoform-resolved long-read single-cell analysis.
steps.input: Platform-specific long-read processing, transcript-resolved single-cell analysis, and sequence-informed interpretation.
steps.output: A unified agent-guided framework that links platform-specific long-read processing to transcript-resolved single-cell analysis and sequence-informed interpretation.
steps.evidence: We next defined the workflow coverage of scIsoAgent across the major stages of isoform-resolved long-read single-cell analysis.

tools.name: BLAZE
tools.description: Barcode and UMI recovery for ONT data.
tools.evidence: For ONT data, scIsoAgent supports barcode and UMI recovery with BLAZE.

tools.name: minimap2
tools.evidence: For ONT data, scIsoAgent supports barcode and UMI recovery with BLAZE, followed by genome alignment using minimap2.

evaluation_metrics.name: LLM-based scoring
evaluation_metrics.purpose: To assess the quality of generated analysis plans.
evaluation_metrics.application: Used in the automated planning evaluation to score plans generated by different LLMs.
evaluation_metrics.evidence: The generated plans were assessed by LLM-based scoring and by specialists in computational biology.

methods.name: Experimental design generation
methods.description: The approach includes producing experimental protocols, configurations, or evaluation strategies.
methods.evidence: We next defined the workflow coverage of scIsoAgent across the major stages of isoform-resolved long-read single-cell analysis.

methods.name: Iterative refinement of Ideas, Hypothesis and Experiment design
methods.description: The system refines experimental designs or hypotheses based on iterative feedback or new data.
methods.evidence: The session management layer preserves computational context across iterative requests, enabling reuse of active sessions and loaded analysis objects.

areas.name: Health Sciences
areas.description: The paper focuses on single-cell RNA-seq analysis, which is relevant to understanding cellular heterogeneity in health and disease.
areas.evidence: To evaluate whether scIsoAgent can support biologically meaningful analysis in a real study setting, we applied it to a published long-read single-cell study of the adult human heart and heart failure.

areas.name: Applied Sciences & Engineering
areas.description: The development of scIsoAgent involves engineering a computational framework for RNA-seq analysis.
areas.evidence: scIsoAgent provides an adaptive workflow framework for ONT and PacBio-based data, connecting heterogeneous long-read single-cell inputs with isoform-level analysis and sequence-informed biological interpretation.

performance_summary.summary: scIsoAgent variants achieved higher overall scores than general-purpose LLMs across three independent LLM-based evaluators, with scIsoAgent-GPT5.4 ranking highest.
performance_summary.evidence: In the automated planning evaluation, scIsoAgent variants achieved higher overall scores than general-purpose LLMs across three independent LLM-based evaluators, with scIsoAgent-GPT5.4 ranking highest.

baselines.name: General-purpose LLMs
baselines.description: Used as a baseline for comparison in planning and execution evaluations.
baselines.evidence: We compared general-purpose LLM baselines with scIsoAgent variants built on different backbone models using the same long-read single-cell transcriptomic analysis prompt.

benchmark_datasets.name: GSE288222
benchmark_datasets.data_description: A published long-read single-cell RNA-seq dataset from the adult human heart and heart failure.
benchmark_datasets.usage: Used for real-data application and reanalysis to evaluate scIsoAgent's performance.
benchmark_datasets.evidence: We evaluated scIsoAgent on a published long-read single-cell RNA-seq dataset from the Gene Expression Omnibus under accession number GSE288222.

evaluation_metrics.purpose: To assess the quality of generated analysis plans.

evaluation_metrics.evidence: The generated plans were assessed by LLM-based scoring and by specialists in computational biology.

benchmark_dataset.name: GSE288222
benchmark_dataset.data_description: A published long-read single-cell RNA-seq dataset from the adult human heart and heart failure.

benchmark_dataset.evidence: We evaluated scIsoAgent on a published long-read single-cell RNA-seq dataset from the Gene Expression Omnibus under accession number GSE288222.

limitations.name: Execution Benchmarks
limitations.description: The execution benchmarks cover representative tasks rather than the full diversity of long-read single-cell workflows.
limitations.evidence: Our execution benchmarks cover representative tasks rather than the full diversity of long-read single-cell workflows, and future evaluations should include broader input types, platforms, and biological use cases.

limitations.name: Planning Assessments
limitations.description: Automated and specialist-based planning assessments may be influenced by selected prompts, scoring rubrics, and evaluator backgrounds.
limitations.evidence: Both automated and specialist-based planning assessments may be influenced by the selected prompts, scoring rubrics, and evaluator backgrounds.

future_directions.name: Broader Input Types
future_directions.description: Future evaluations should include broader input types, platforms, and biological use cases.
future_directions.evidence: Our execution benchmarks cover representative tasks rather than the full diversity of long-read single-cell workflows, and future evaluations should include broader input types, platforms, and biological use cases.

future_directions.name: Experimental Validation
future_directions.description: Sequence-derived predictions should be interpreted as hypothesis-generating evidence rather than direct functional validation.
future_directions.evidence: Such predictions require experimental validation, but they offer a principled way to prioritize isoforms for downstream functional studies.

resource_link.evidence: The codebase for scIsoAgent is publicly available at https://github.com/zczali4403/scIsoAgent.
